sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 80

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 80:1
items.html:
(to the question) Salik beRe'uta
The Zivug made on a discernment of Masach in which there is only Aviut of the discernment of Keter — which is only the root discernment of Aviut — is called a Zivug of Re'uta [desire], or Salik beRe'uta ["it arose in the desire"]. This is because the Keter is root to all the desires in the four discernments, as is known. (Item 1)
